name: Figs
recipeIngredient: An average of 16 pounds is needed per canner load of 7 quarts; an average of 11 pounds is needed per canner load of 9 pints; or 2-1/2 pounds yields 1 quart.
recipeInstructions:
1. Select firm, ripe, uncracked figs.
2. The mature color depends on the variety.
3. Avoid overripe figs with very soft flesh.
4. Wash figs thoroughly in clean water and drain.
5. Do not peel or remove stems.
6. Cover figs with water and boil for 2 minutes.
7. Drain.
8. Gently boil figs in light syrup for 5 minutes.
9. Add 2 tablespoons bottled lemon juice per quart or 1 tablespoon per pint to the jars, or add 1/2 teaspoon citric acid per quart or 1/4 teaspoon per pint to the jars.
10. Fill jars with hot figs and cooking syrup, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
11. Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1.
Table 1. Recommended process time for Figs in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Hot
Jar Size: Pints
- Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 45 min
- Process Time at Altitudes of 1,001 - 3,000 ft: 50 min
- Process Time at Altitudes of 3,001 - 6,000 ft: 55 min
- Process Time at Altitudes above 6,000 ft: 60 min
Style of Pack: Hot
Jar Size: Quarts
- Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 50 min
- Process Time at Altitudes of 1,001 - 3,000 ft: 55 min
- Process Time at Altitudes of 3,001 - 6,000 ft: 60 min
- Process Time at Altitudes above 6,000 ft: 65 min
